cover_letter_preview:
Dear Hiring Team,

The iCIMS ATS Administrator role at Enterprise Solution Inc. caught my attention because the core of the work, keeping a system running cleanly, supporting the people who depend on it, and building documentation that makes the whole team more consistent, maps directly to what I did at BEAT THE BOMB. My background is in technical operations and systems administration, not ATS-specific administration, but the operational habits the role depends on are ones I've built in production.

At BEAT THE BOMB, I owned the help desk ticket system, authored SOPs and runbooks that let junior staff resolve recurring issues without escalation, and supported user so members were operational with minimal friction. That documentation work wasn't a side task, it was how I kept a 20-to-30-person running consistently when I wasn't the only one touching the systems. The same instinct applies here: if a recruiter hits a broken workflow or a report pulls inconsistent data, the fix needs to be documented so it doesn't happen again.

On the reporting and data side, I tracked operational KPIs and generated reports to support decision-making across shift operations, and I coordinated across d…
